top_signals.title: Russian Refinery Runs Plunge to Lowest in More Than Two Decades
top_signals.summary: A wave of Ukrainian attacks has pushed Russian refining runs to the lowest in more than 21 years, deepening a domestic fuel crunch and further squeezing the global market.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Oil and LNG Tankers Go Dark Again as Hormuz Crisis Deepens
top_signals.summary: Oil and LNG tankers are back to switching off transponders when transiting the Strait of Hormuz as the latest escalation in the region and last week’s Iranian attacks on commercial ships prompted vessel operators to brave the chokepoint unobserved. Total tanker traffic collapsed on Sunday to just six vessels managing to clear the Strait of Hormuz, all in the so-called dark mode to avoid detection, Bloomberg News reported on Monday, citing preliminary data by Kpler it had analyzed. The re-emergence of the dark-mode transit is in stark contrast…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Wall Street Mounts Pushback on Trillion-Dollar Stablecoin Boom
top_signals.summary: For years, banks largely watched stablecoins evolve from a niche cryptocurrency product into a payments network moving tens of trillions of dollars annually. Now they’re dusting off the same collaborative playbook that produced Zelle, betting shared infrastructure is the best way to stop digital dollars from encroaching further on their business.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Wimbledon: HMRC set to slap Sinner and Noskova with £1.6m tax bill
top_signals.summary: Wimbledon winners Jannik Sinner and Linda Noskova have been slapped with whopping multi-million pound tax bills after bagging £3.6m apiece at the All England Club. Sinner defended his title with a four-set win over French Open champion Alexander Zverev on Sunday while Noskova toppled Czech compatriot Karolina Muchova in three sets on Saturday. Both picked [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: New Foreign Direct Investment in the United States, 2025
top_signals.summary: Expenditures by foreign direct investors to acquire, establish, or expand U.S. businesses totaled $232.2 billion in 2025, according to preliminary statistics released today by the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis. Expenditures increased $76.8 billion, or 49.5 percent, from 2024 levels. As in previous years, acquisitions of existing U.S. businesses accounted for most of the expenditures. Acquisition expenditures in 2025 were $218.4 billion, expenditures to establish new U.S. businesses were $4.6 billion, and expenditures to expand existing foreign-owned businesses were $9.2 billion. Planned total expenditures, which include both first-year and planned future expenditures, were $284.5 billion. Full Text
